candidate_problems.text: Can a precise, generally covariant mechanism be formulated in which vacuum energy contributions are sequestered by a genuinely global constraint or feedback rule, while remaining consistent with local QFT, diffeomorphism invariance, stress-energy conservation, and known tests of GR?

What observable consequences would distinguish 'global consistency selection' of the cosmological constant from ordinary ΛCDM with a fundamental constant or from dynamical dark-energy models—e.g., in cosmic expansion history, structure formation, horizon-scale correlations, or sensitivity to global boundary conditions?

Is the public-good / overcounting analogy merely heuristic, or can it be made into a rigorous information-theoretic or systems-level derivation showing exactly why naive mode-by-mode vacuum-energy summation double-counts non-gravitating degrees of freedom and what the correct coarse-grained gravitating variable should be?
